title: GUIDELINES FOR THE EXCHANGE OF INFORMATION BETWEEN COUNTRIES ON REJECTIONS OF IMPORTED FOOD

sections.section: GUIDELINES FOR THE EXCHANGE OF INFORMATION BETWEEN COUNTRIES ON REJECTIONS OF IMPORTED FOOD
sections.summary: Heading only: title of the guidelines for exchange of information between countries on rejections of imported food.
Document identifier and history: CAC/GL 25-1997, adopted 1997 and revised 2016.

Introduction sets scope for structured information exchange when imported food is refused entry due to non‑compliance related to food safety or fair trade and directs use of CAC/GL 19‑1995 for food safety emergencies; it requires appropriate information sharing with exporting authorities (or embassies), access to evidence for investigations, reporting of investigation outcomes if requested, consultation between importer/exporter and competent authorities on actions, and notes FAO/WHO assistance if requested.

General considerations list grounds for rejection (food safety risk, compromise during handling/storage/transport, misrepresentation/fraud), require exchange of information to enable clarifications and corrective/preventive measures, recommend providing information to exporting competent authorities, grant exporting authorities reasonable access to evidence and results of investigations, permit notification for repeated non‑safety failures, encourage bilateral discussions, and call for minimizing restrictions on disclosure to exporting countries.

Detailed information prescribes that exchanges should be electronic where possible, transparent, structured, timely and in an agreed language; reasons for rejection must reference contravened regulations and detail sampling/analytical methods, laboratory results and contaminant identities/levels (including maximum permitted levels or organism/toxin identity where no level exists), and should specify contraventions of additive or compositional standards or lack of evidence of approved establishments.

sections.section: STANDARD FORMAT FOR EXCHANGE OF INFORMATION BETWEEN COUNTRIES ON REJECTIONS OF IMPORTED FOOD
sections.summary: Provides a standard format for information exchange on rejected imported food, specifying the categories of information to be supplied as available (identification of food, importation details, decision details, reasons for rejection and supporting evidence, and action taken).

Specifies required product identification details for rejected consignments, including description and quantity, HS code, packaging type/size, lot identification, transport/container details, identification marks, certificate number/copy if applicable, and names/addresses/establishment numbers of manufacturer/producer/exporter.
Requires importation information such as exporter and importer names and contacts, container and shipping details including port of origin/destination, and the date presented for entry.

Requires details of the rejection decision including whether the whole or part of the consignment was rejected, name and address of the competent authority making the decision, date of decision, and contact details of the authority that can provide further information on the reason for rejection.

Lists possible reasons for rejection that must be specified with supporting evidence, including biological/microbiological contamination, chemical contamination (e.g. heavy metals), pesticide/veterinary residues, radionuclides, incorrect or misleading labelling, compositional or additive non‑conformity, unacceptable organoleptic quality, temperature noncompliance, technical/physical defects, incomplete/incorrect certification, unapproved origin, adulteration, and other reasons.

Specifies actions to report following rejection, such as destruction, holding pending reconditioning or documentation rectification, permitting non‑human use, conditional re‑export, notifying the importer and exporting country authorities/embassy, notifying other likely destination countries, or other actions.
